user_query:
I’m planning our next gentle, insect-friendly trek with the club (I could never swat a fly, let alone own a swatter). Please inspect the local records under sightings/ and notes/ and compile a concise, verifiable summary of sensitive species by trail, then update my draft brief and prepare meeting notes.

Inputs to use:
- sightings/records.csv
- notes/trail_notes.md
- docs/club_brief_draft.md (to be rewritten in place)

Definitions and constraints:
- Treat a species as sensitive if, in sightings/records.csv, its status is one of: UK BAP priority, protected, or priority (case-insensitive); OR if it appears on a line starting with "RARE:" under that trail in notes/trail_notes.md.
- Species names are case-insensitive; deduplicate across CSV and notes.
- For notes/trail_notes.md, the species on a RARE line is exactly the text after "RARE:" on that line, trimmed.
- Compute for each trail: the set of unique sensitive species and its count. Include a trail if it appears in the CSV; if a trail appears in notes with RARE lines but not in the CSV, include it as well (not expected with current files, but handle it generally).
- When listing species, sort alphabetically by common name.
- For tie-breaking when ranking trails by sensitive_count, sort alphabetically by trail name.

Deliverables:
1) Create output/sensitive_trails.csv with header:
   trail,sensitive_species,sensitive_count,sources
   where:
   - sensitive_species is a semicolon-separated alphabetical list of unique sensitive species for that trail.
   - sensitive_count is the number of unique sensitive species for that trail.
   - sources is a semicolon-separated set drawn from {CSV,Notes}: include CSV if at least one sensitive species for that trail comes from sightings/records.csv; include Notes if at least one sensitive species comes from a RARE line. List sources alphabetically.

2) Edit docs/club_brief_draft.md in place to replace placeholders with current facts derived from the inputs:
   - Update the Date: line to the latest date present in sightings/records.csv.
   - Replace the Summary: TBD with a single sentence: "Based on records through <LATEST_DATE>, <N> trails have sensitive sightings." where N is the count of trails with sensitive species.
   - In the "Trails under review:" list, for each listed trail replace "sensitive species: [UNKNOWN]; notes: TODO" with:
     "sensitive species: <comma-separated alphabetical list from your computation, or None recorded>; notes: <RARE note present|No RARE note>"
     (Use "RARE note present" if that trail has at least one RARE line in notes/trail_notes.md; otherwise "No RARE note"). Leave other sections as-is.

3) Prepare output/meeting_notes.md with two sections:
   - Decisions Needed: a three-item bullet list naming the top 3 trails by sensitive_count from output/sensitive_trails.csv (descending by count, tie-break alphabetically) prefixed with "- " (if fewer than 3 exist, list all available). Use trail names only.
   - Action Items: one bullet per trail in that top-3 list formatted exactly as:
     "- <TRAIL>: agree observation protocol and signage (owner: <ROLE>)"
     Assign roles in this order: 1st trail = Lead Walker; 2nd = Recorder; 3rd = Signage Volunteer.

Assumptions:
- Work only with the provided files in sightings/, notes/, and docs/. Do not invent species or trails.
- All outputs must be saved to the specified paths, and docs/club_brief_draft.md must be updated in place.

This should give me a clean, gentle plan that keeps our treasured insects undisturbed on the trek. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
